response.justification: Candidate has strong general engineering breadth, testing discipline, and relevant geospatial exposure through GISCollective, but lacks the core technical requirement for this role: modern C++ or Rust experience. C++ work is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago, and there is no Rust experience listed. Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which do not directly transfer to a routing-tile pipeline written in C++. Worth a screen only if the team is open to candidates who would need to ramp up substantially on the primary language.
strengths: Founder of an open-source geospatial platform (GISCollective) with real GIS pipeline experience
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, low-latency systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Broad polyglot background (C/C++, D, TypeScript, Java) and willingness to work across the stack
concerns: C++ experience is brief and dated (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015); no recent commercial C++ work
No Rust experience listed, despite it being the acceptable alternative
No direct navigation/routing-tile or large-scale distributed data-pipeline experience

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ or Rust experience
No navigation systems or routing pipeline experience
